summary: A brief biographical sketch of Virginia M.-Y. Lee, highlighting her transition from studying piano to becoming a leading neuroscientist researching misfolded proteins. It also includes an anecdote from a colleague illustrating her direct, unfiltered communication style in the lab.

signature_quotes.text: I was pretty good at it [piano], but it wasn’t really for me.
signature_quotes.context: On deciding to convince her mother to let her study science instead of music.

signature_quotes.text: Get out! I'm in an interview, can you see what I'm doing?!
signature_quotes.context: Yelling at her husband and research partner, John Trojanowski, when he interrupted an interview to offer her a piece of fruit.
